back_translation: When men began to multiply on the face of the earth, and daughters were born to them,
the sons of God saw that the daughters of men were beautiful, and they took as wives any they chose.
The LORD said, "My Spirit will not contend with man forever, for he is flesh; his days shall be 120 years."
The Nephilim were on the earth in those days—and afterward as well—when the sons of God came to the daughters of men, and they bore children to them. They were the heroes of old, men of renown.
The LORD saw that the wickedness of man on the earth was great, and that every thought from his heart was only evil all the time.
The LORD was grieved that He had made man on the earth, and He was deeply grieved in His heart.
The LORD said, "I will sweep away the man whom I created from the face of the earth—both men, livestock, crawling creatures, and birds of the sky—because I am grieved that I made them."
But Noah received grace in the eyes of the LORD.
This is the account of Noah. Noah was a righteous and blameless man among his contemporaries; he walked with God.
Noah had three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
The earth was corrupt before God, and full of violence.
God looked at the earth, and behold, it was corrupt, because all flesh on the earth had corrupted their ways.
God said to Noah, "I have decided to bring an end to all living creatures, because the earth is full of violence because of them. Behold, I will destroy them and the earth.
Make for yourself an ark of gopher wood; make rooms in the ark, and coat it with pitch inside and outside.
This is how you shall build it: the ark shall be 300 cubits long, 50 cubits wide, and 30 cubits high.
Make a roof for the ark, finishing it within a cubit at the top. Place a door in the side of the ark, and make lower, middle, and upper decks.
Behold, I will bring floodwaters upon the earth to destroy every kind of creature with breath under the heavens. Everything on the earth will perish.
But I will establish My covenant with you. You shall enter the ark—you, your sons, your wife, and your sons' wives.
You shall bring two of every living creature into the ark, male and female, to keep them alive with you.
Birds according to their kind, livestock according to their kind, and every kind of crawling creature on the ground—two of each will come to you to be kept alive.
You shall take for yourself every kind of food that is eaten, and store it. It shall be food for you and for the animals."
Noah did everything God had commanded him; thus he did.
